title: Node Group System (Collapsible Subgraphs with Exposed Parameters)
description: Implement the node group system that allows collapsing a subgraph into a single reusable node with exposed parameters. This is the core of the semantic hierarchy -- a 'Hand' group contains 'Palm', 'Thumb', 'Finger' sub-groups, each with their own exposed parameters. Groups can nest arbitrarily. A group is defined by a NodeGraph with GroupInput/GroupOutput nodes marking its interface. When used in a parent graph, the group appears as a single node whose inputs/outputs match the GroupInput/GroupOutput ports. Groups are serializable to JSON as named, reusable templates. The LLM generates these groups as the DSL representation of semantic parts.
definition-of-done: 1. NodeGroup class wraps a NodeGraph with a name, description, and interface (inputs/outputs derived from GroupInput/GroupOutput nodes). 2. NodeGroupInstance can be placed in a parent graph and behaves like a single node. 3. Groups can be nested (a group containing another group instance). 4. Exposed parameters from GroupInput are editable from the parent scope. 5. JSON serialization/deserialization of groups works, including nested groups. 6. A library/registry of saved groups can be loaded by name.
testing-plan: 1. Create a 'ScaledCube' group: GroupInput(size) -> MeshCube(size) -> Transform(scale=size) -> GroupOutput(mesh). 2. Instantiate it in a parent graph with size=2, verify output is a 2x cube. 3. Nest: create a 'TwoCubes' group containing two ScaledCube instances, verify both appear in output. 4. Serialize TwoCubes to JSON, deserialize, verify identical output. 5. Change an exposed parameter, re-evaluate, verify output updates.
todos: Define NodeGroup class with name, description, inner NodeGraph, and derived interface
Define NodeGroupInstance that implements MeshNode using a NodeGroup as its backing implementation
Wire NodeGroupInstance.evaluate() to evaluate the inner graph with inputs mapped to GroupInput
Implement JSON serialization of groups (inline the inner graph)
Implement a GroupLibrary that stores named groups and resolves them by name during deserialization
Add validation: every group must have exactly one GroupInput and one GroupOutput
unknowns: Whether groups should support multiple outputs or just one
Whether to support group versioning for when the LLM modifies a group that is instanced multiple times
